text: **Anna:** DC is a {popular|人気の|/ˈpɒpjələr/|A popular restaurant in town.} city for {marathons|マラソン大会|/məˈræθən/|Many runners join marathons.}.
**Anna:** A {marathon|マラソン大会|/məˈræθən/|A marathon is 42.195 kilometers.} is a long race.
**Woman:** Many marathons raise money for {charities|慈善団体|/ˈtʃærəti/|Charities help people in need.}.
**Anna:** A marathon is a good {fitness|体力|/ˈfɪtnəs/|Fitness is important for health.} goal too.
**Anna:** I want to {challenge|挑戦する|/ˈtʃælɪndʒ/|I challenge myself to learn.} myself in a marathon and maybe win a {medal|メダル|/ˈmedl/|He won a gold medal.}!
**Ashley:** Do you want to sit down, Anna?
**Anna:** I didn't know you like to run.
**Ashley:** In fact, this weekend I will {run|走る|/rʌn/|I run every morning.} in my first {marathon|マラソン大会|/məˈræθən/|A marathon is 42.195 kilometers.}.
**Ashley:** You are running in a marathon?
**Anna:** Why do you ask like that?
**Ashley:** What do you know about {running|走ること/競争|/ˈrʌnɪŋ/|Running is good exercise.} in a marathon?
**Anna:** That there's a lot of {running|走ること/競争} and sometimes you can win a {medal|メダル/賞|/ˈmedl/|He won a gold medal.}
**Ashley:** And how long have you been training?
**Anna:** I've been {training|訓練する/練習する|/ˈtreɪnɪŋ/|Athletes train every day.} for an hour, no, an hour and seven minutes.
**Ashley:** {Training|訓練/練習} a little every day is a good {habit|習慣|/ˈhæbɪt/|It's a good habit to read.} to get into.
**Anna:** Thanks for the {advice|助言/提案|/ædˈvaɪs/|I need your advice.}, Ashley.
**Anna:** But I'm running in a special race
**Anna:** I don't remember the name, but the website said everyone gets a medal.
**Anna:** This is our {model|モデル/見本|/ˈmɑːdl/|This car is a new model.}, six-year-old, please pay it.
**Anna:** I'm here to {enter|入る/参加する|/ˈentər/|Enter the competition.} the race.
**Woman:** But ma'am, you can't enter the race.
**Anna:** How am I going to meet my {goal|目標|/ɡoʊl/|My goal is to win.}?
**Woman:** Ma'am, this race is for {children|子供たち|/ˈtʃɪldrən/|Children play in the park.}.
**Woman:** You can't run with the children.
**Anna:** Children children That's perfect I just might win No ma'am you really can't run with the children I'm sorry of course I was only thinking of my goal
**Woman:** Well, you could help us with our goal, which is to raise money for sick children.
**Woman:** Would you like to help us?
**Anna:** {Awesome|素晴らしい/素晴らしい！|/ˈɔːsəm/|That's an awesome performance!}!
**Anna:** I've just {found|見つけた/発見した|/faʊnd/|I found a treasure.} my new {goal|目標/目的|/ɡoʊl/|My goal is to be a doctor.}.
**Anna:** And I get to {wear|着る/身につける|/wer/|I wear a blue shirt.} a {medal|メダル/勲章|/ˈmedl/|She won a gold medal.}.
